title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 3)
statement_text: Every graph $H$ satisfies the Erdős-Hajnal property, i.e., there exists a constant $\delta > 0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $n$ vertices contains either a clique or an independent set of size $n^\delta$.
context_text: The Erdős-Hajnal conjecture asserts that forbidding a fixed graph $H$ as an induced subgraph forces large homogeneous sets, in sharp contrast with random graphs where the maximum clique/independent set is only $O(\log n)$. The conjecture has been verified for all graphs on at most four vertices, the bull, cliques, and graphs constructible from them by substitution.
paper_contribution: Proves that a constructive version of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture implies the improved approximation conjecture (Conjecture 5), and shows the improved approximation property is closed under graph substitution, establishing Conjecture 5 for many graphs $H$ (e.g., $P_6$-free, fork-free) where the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture remains open.
paper_title: An algorithmic weakening of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture

title: Conjecture 5 (Improved Approximation Conjecture)
statement_text: Every graph $H$ satisfies the improved approximation property, i.e., there exists a constant $\varepsilon > 0$ such that MIS admits a (randomized) $n^{1-\varepsilon}$-approximation polynomial algorithm on every $H$-free $n$-vertex graph $G$.
context_text: This conjecture is introduced as an algorithmic weakening of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture. It states informally that the inapproximability of MIS in general graphs can always be beaten in any proper hereditary class. It is strictly weaker than the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture, since MIS is polynomial in $P_6$-free graphs while it is open whether $P_5$ satisfies the Erdős-Hajnal property.
paper_title: An algorithmic weakening of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture

title: Question: optimal approximation exponent for MIS in H-free graphs
statement_text: For a given $H$, what is the largest $\varepsilon > 0$ such that MIS admits an $O(n^{1-\varepsilon})$-approximation algorithm in $H$-free graphs?
context_text: After establishing that many graphs $H$ satisfy the improved approximation property, the authors ask how to optimise the approximation ratio. They investigate this in Sections 3 and 4, proving e.g. a $\sqrt{OPT}$-approximation in $C_4$-free graphs and a tight inapproximability bound in triangle-free and high-girth graphs.
notes: Posed as an open research direction in running prose in the 'Results and organization' section; partially investigated within the paper but not fully resolved.
paper_title: An algorithmic weakening of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture
